text: In this video, we present a robotic transcendental excision of a rectal leiomyoma with DaVinci XI system. This was a fifty-eight years old lady diagnosed with a one centimeter tumoral lesion located at the upper rectum. Preoperative rectal endosynography revealed a submucosal lesion suggesting a gastrointestinal

The patient is positioned in a prone jackknife position for the procedure. A gel point pad serves as a single port trocar, fixed into the anal canal, with three 8mm robotic ports placed. The rectum is insufflated with CO₂, and robotic arms are docked from the left side. The surgeon marks the resection margin using robotic superficial tip cautery. The mucosa over the anterior rectal wall lesion is gently grasped with a Maryland bipolar forceps, and the lesion is excised using superficial tip cautery.

The surgeon closes the defect on the rectal wall using a continuous three OV lock suture, following the excision of the lesion. The surgical field shows minimal blood loss, approximately three milliliters.
